titleStmt.title: Taishō Tripiṭaka
The Taishō Revised Tripiṭaka
Inscription on Faith in Mind
Taishō Tripiṭaka, Electronic version, No. 2010, Inscription on Faith in Mind
Taishō Revised Tripiṭaka, Digital Edition, No. 2010, Inscription on Faith in Mind
titleStmt.author: Composed by Sengcan of the Sui

distributor.name: Chinese Buddhist Electronic Text Association Foundation (CBETA)

availability.p: Available for non-commercial use when distributed with this header intact.

projectDesc.p: Text as provided by Mr. Hsiao Chen-Kuo, Text as provided by Electronic Buddhadharma Society (EBS), Text as provided by Anonymous from USA
Provided by the Venerable Xiao Zhengguo, provided by the Buddhist Computer Information Repository Merit Society, provided by a certain venerable in North America

punctuation.p: Punctuation as in the original edition

revisionDesc.change: CWRay Chou (Zhou Bangxin)P4 to P5 conversion by p4top5a.py, intended for publication
CW (ed.) Created initial TEI XML version with BASICX.BAT (00/01/24)

body.note:
Machine translated by theksepyro
Some of the first lines retranslated by ewk on the podcast.

lg.l: The Supreme Way is not difficult. Just disdain value-creating discrimination. Just do not be averse or attached.
Clearly and thoroughly understooda hair’s breadth of difference. Heaven and earth are far apart
If you wish it to appear before you, do not continue to follow along or oppose. Opposing and agreeing contend with each other
This is called the disease of the mind. Not knowing the profound meaningin vain you labor in thoughts of stillness
Perfectly round like the great voidlacking nothing and nothing in excessindeed because of grasping and rejecting
therefore it is not thusdo not chase conditioned existencedo not abide in empty forbearance
With a single kind of even mindnaturally all is extinguishedstopping movement and returning to stillness
stopping again only increases movementmerely clinging to the two sideshow could one know the single kind
The single kind is not penetrated. In both places the work is lost. Dismiss being and there is no non-being.
Following emptiness yet turning away from emptiness. Much speech and much thought. Turn ever more out of accord
Cut off words and cut off thoughtnowhere is there that is not penetratedreturn to the root and obtain the meaning
Following illumination, you lose the sourcein an instant turn the light backit surpasses the previous emptiness
The former emptiness undergoes changeall because of deluded viewsthere is no need to seek the true
Only cease from viewslet not the two views abidetake care not to pursue them
As soon as there is right and wrongconfusion arises and the mind is lostthe two arise from the one
but do not cling even to the onewhen the one mind is not bornthe myriad dharmas are without fault
Without fault, without dharmasnot born, not mindthe subject can follow conditions and cease
conditions follow the subject and sink awayconditions arise from the subject as conditionsthe subject arises from conditions as subject
If you wish to know these two aspectstheir origin is a single emptinessthe one emptiness is the same as the two
Equally containing the myriad formswithout seeing fine or coarsehow could there be partiality or faction
The Great Way is vast in essenceneither easy nor difficultwith narrow views there is doubt and suspicion
The more hurried, the more delayedgrasping it, you lose the measurethe mind enters a deviant path
Let it go and it is naturalthe essence neither goes nor abidesaccord with your nature and unite with the Way
At ease and free from vexationto fix the mind is to stray from the trueSinking into dullness is not good
If it is not good, it wearies the spiritwhat use are estrangement and intimacyif you wish to approach the One Vehicle
do not hate the six sense-objectswhen the six sense-objects are not hatedthey are the same as right awakening
The wise are without contrivancethe foolish bind themselvesthe dharma has no different dharma
yet delusion gives rise to attachmentusing mind to seek mindis this not a great mistake
Delusion gives rise to quiet and disturbancein awakening there is no liking or dislikingall the two sides
are foolishly weighed and measureddreams, illusions, empty flowerswhy trouble to grasp them
Gain and loss, right and wronglet them all go at onceif the eye does notsleep
all dreams will naturally ceaseif the mind makes no distinctionsthe myriad dharmas are of one suchness
The one suchness is profound in essenceabruptly forgetting conditionsthe myriad dharmas are viewed equally
returning again to what is naturalobliterating the reasons whyit cannot be compared by analogy
Stopping movement, there is no movementin movement, there is no stoppingwhen the two are not established
how could the one existultimately and to the utmostnot abiding by rules
Awaken the mind of equalityall activities ceasedoubt is completely purified
Right faith is even and uprightnothing is retainedthere is nothing to remember
Empty and luminous, self-sowithout exerting mental efforta place beyond thought
Consciousness and feeling are hard to fathomtrue suchness is the dharma-realmwithout other and without self
To accord swiftlyjust speak of non-dualityin non-duality all is the same
There is nothing it does not embracethe wise of the ten directionsall enter this teaching
The teaching is neither hurried nor delayedone thought is ten thousand yearsten thousand years are one thoughtthere is no here or not-here
The ten directions are right before the eyesthe extremely small is the same as the greatdelusion ends and boundaries vanish
The extremely great is the same as the smallno boundaries are seenbeing is precisely non-being
Non-being is precisely beingif it is not thusyou need not hold to it
One is allall is oneif only you can be thus
why worry that it will not becompletefaith and mind are not twonon-dual are faith and mind
The path of words is cut offnotpast present or future

cb:juan.cb:jhead: The End of the Xinxin Ming

app.lem: A thought in ten thousand years

p.note: A thought in ten thousand years【Da】，〔－〕【Jia】
【Original】Printed in the first year of Keian, copy held at the University of Religion,【Jia】Printed in the nineteenth year of Kyōhō, copy held at Komazawa University
